Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted on PLAINTIFF BARBARA COHEN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendant Unums Deposition Subpoena Seeking The Production Of Plaintiffs Marriage And Family Therapist Naomi Fairs Records And For Protective Order as follows:

The Motion is GRANTED, in part and DENIED in part: The subpoena shall be quashed as to any and all records prior to defendant's denial of disability benefits on August 5, 2016. Only records after that date shall be produced, as they are the only such records with the required nexus to plaintiff's claims of emotional distress resulting from the denial of disability benefits.

The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to Judge Pro Tem David M. Helbraun for review and signature.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-24-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Ryan M. Opgenorth of the Pillsbury Coleman, Tel. No. 1-415-433-8000
appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Robert E. Hess of the Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP, Tel. No. 1-213-
614-7318 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Unum Group and Provident Life and Accident Insurance Company's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff Barbara Cohen's second cause of action for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing and request for punitive damages is denied as to both issues. There are triable disputes whether defendants' denial of policy benefits to Ms. Cohen was: 1) unreasonable and 2) done in conscious and willful disregard of Ms. Cohen's rights under the policy that subjected Ms. Cohen to cruel and unjust hardship and was despicable. Ms. Cohen's ability to successfully carry out her duties as a hearing officer does not negate the possibility that defendants' denial of policy benefits to her was unreasonable and/or taken in a manner that would allow a reasonable jury to find that defendants acted with malice and/or oppression within the meaning of Civil Code 3294(c)(1) and (2).Viewed in the light most favorable to Ms. Cohen, the evidence submitted by Ms. Cohen could support a determination by a reasonable jury that defendants knowingly, without regard to the effect it would have on Ms. Cohen and to save money chose to disregard the medically and vocationally well-founded views of Ms. Cohen and her health care professionals and their own vocational expert to assert without any factual support that Ms. Cohen was able to perform the material duties of a trial attorney and they did so without considering recent medical information that they knew existed but did not have. Defendants' objections to paragraph 26 of the declaration of Ryan Opgenorth and exhibits 1-6 of that declaration and exhibits 1-28 to Ms. Cohen's request for judicial notice are sustained. All other objections to evidence made by defendants are overruled. Ms. Cohen's objections to the evidence submitted with the reply papers are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
